mnemonics.title: Selection Statement Order
mnemonics.content: If Ever Hungry, Eat In Order (IEHEIO) - Remember the sequence: if → else if → else. The first true condition gets executed in order.

mnemonics.title: Loop Types
mnemonics.content: Forget While Doing Work (FWDW) - For loop (counted), While loop (pre-test), Do-while loop (post-test)

mnemonics.title: Switch Break Rule
mnemonics.content: Switch Breaks Prevent Falls - Always use break in switch cases to prevent accidental fall-through to next case

mnemonics.title: Jump Statements
mnemonics.content: Breakfast, Coffee, Return (BCR) - Break (exit loop), Continue (skip iteration), Return (exit method)

acronyms.usage: <% if %> <% else %> <% end if %> pattern in JSP scriptlets

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a railway switch - condition determines which track (code branch) the execution train takes
Picture a water faucet - loop runs while water (condition) is flowing. Faucet handle = loop condition
Visualize an elevator - each case is a floor button. Break statements are elevator doors closing after reaching floor
See it as a clock: initialization (set time), condition (while clock is running), iteration (ticking mechanism)
Traffic signals: Break (red light), Continue (yellow 'prepare to stop'), Return (end of road sign)

analogies.analogy: Like a fork in the road - GPS recalculates route based on 'if' there's traffic, 'else' takes default path
Washing machine cycle - initialize (load clothes), test (while dirty), execute (wash), update (rinse count)
Vending machine - each button (case) gives different snack. Pressing break is like getting your item without pressing other buttons
Retry button on ATM - do transaction first, then check if you want to continue (while 'another transaction?')
Russian nesting dolls - each decision (if/loop) contains smaller decisions inside, requiring careful alignment
